title: Item count in dashboard number widget
excerpt: Sometimes you just need to know how the total count of items in a specific group or status on your dashboard. Ex: 78 items not in status completed, etc.

posts.author: jogo (Jordan G)
posts.cookedHtml: Sometimes you just need to know how the total count of items in a specific group or status on your dashboard. Ex: 78 items not in status completed, etc.

posts.author: PedroM (Pedro Malaga - monday.com Certified Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jogo,

If you need it, you can also retrieve that information easily using a chart in a dashboard. Once you create the chart with the information you need, you can filter by group or status so you will only display that set of information.
Hope it helps,

posts.author: jogo (Jordan G)
posts.cookedHtml:
Appreciate the workaround idea Pedro, but then you’re stuck with a single bar or solid pie with a tiny number & % in the legend, when all you need is a count.

image696×382 49.1 KB

posts.author: PedroM (Pedro Malaga - monday.com Certified Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ok I see, considering that approach, once you have the number you can filter the widget by any column, so you can filter by status or group to get the value you need.
If you need another kind of filter that is not available you can use the formulas to create it (IF and SWITCH works very well for that).
Another approach to get your counter would be to create an “Auto ID” column and a formula column. In the formula column you can insert the “Auto ID” so you will get a unique ID for each item that you will be able use to count in the number widget.

posts.author: jogo (Jordan G)
posts.cookedHtml: Much appreciated but the goal is to not have to add overhead columns if not absolutely needed…

posts.author: bufordm (Maggie Buford)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @jogo did you ever find a better way to count items? I am running into this problem as well.

posts.author: jogo (Jordan G)
posts.cookedHtml: Not yet, without workarounds. My understanding is this is on the roadmap under craftsmanship / quality of life improvements for a future update.

posts.author: DylanDowns (Dylan Downs)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to +1 this request.

posts.author: MelissaCLark (Melissa Clark)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 to this request. Hopefully soon!

posts.author: Jemima (Jemima Stewart )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi There, I am looking for this at present, has it been resolved yet?

posts.author: jogo (Jordan G)
posts.cookedHtml: Not yet, you still have to have a number or formula column to make this work. Which is fine if your board has/needs a number column anyway, but artificial overhead if you don’t want/need a number column.

posts.author: PixelBakery (Jordan Lambrecht)
posts.cookedHtml:
This shows up first in Google, so I wanted to provide my approach.

  1. Create a numbers column
  2. Setup an automation to set an item’s number column to 1 every time a new item is created
  3. Hide the column so we don’t have to see it (idk if locking it works with automation, but that might be nice too)
  4. Create a dashboard view and add the numbers widget. I have four in the board I’m working in: one for the sum of all groups, and three for the sum of each individual group

posts.author: ryanrs (Ryan Sutherland)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this still not possible without creating a numbers column only to get a filtered count of items on a dashboard?

posts.author: sabimorr (Sabia Morrison)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d really like to be able to do a count on overall items on a board and also on individual groups. Any advance yet?

posts.author: sbullman (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any update on this? I’d really love to see this feature! Excel has had the automatically visible sum/count at the bottom of the application for 15+ years.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @sbullman - a couple of weeks ago a noted this feature was available for number widgets including items and subitems:

Thanks!
Mark

posts.author: sbullman (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml: This doesn’t work in a table, does it?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

We can confirm that this feature is now supported in the platform.

Screen Shot 2023-04-19 at 10.07.13 am648×538 17.2 KB

Please let us know if you run into any issues

posts.author: mb16 (Mitzi Benavides)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there! I don’t see the option to then select which items we want to count. For example, if I want a count of unique company names listed under a COMPANY column, how would I go about that?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you confirm the type of column the company column is?

posts.author: mb16 (Mitzi Benavides)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s a short text column. I’m looking to count the number of unique company names.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for clarifying! At this time, it isn’t possible to customise which specific items you’d like to count based on another column in the Numbers widget - in your case, the company column. I apologise for the setback here!

That said, I am interested if you could instead use a chart widget (specifically a stacked bar chart) to achieve this?

Screen Shot 2023-07-12 at 10.19.06 am1105×540 14.1 KB

In this case, it is not only counting the items, but also categorising the item count based on the company…

Let me know what you think!

posts.author: swheeler (Sydney Wheeler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I am also looking for a way to show the number of unique company names from a text column in a number widget on my dashboard. The bar graph solution doesn’t really do that becuase you would still have to manually count the number of bars in the chart. Is there a way to show the number of unique values in a column ? I can change the column to another type if necessary (dropdown menu, status…).

Thanks!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Sydney,

Hm, I am interested if you could utilise the widget filters to pull this specific data? You can then save the filter so that the widget permanently displays the uniques values in the column?

Screen Shot 2023-07-14 at 10.12.05 am1454×1162 57.7 KB

posts.author: swheeler (Sydney Wheeler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca, thanks for getting back to me on this.

Filtering by company won’t work for me here, because what I’m looking for is the total number of company names that appears in the column. With this filter as with the bar chart, I would see the total number of lines, so a company that appears in the table 3 times will be counted 3 times, whereas I need it to only be counted once.

For more context, I have a table where I track employee volunteer engagement. Each line corresponds with a specific volunteering activity, and indicates the volunteer and which company they work for. Therefore, a company can appear multiple times in my table. I need to be able to calculate the number of companies who participated in activities, which means counting each company only once, regardless of how many times it appears in my table.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @swheeler,
You can use the Duplicates and Uniques app for that. Basically create a template that tracks unique items and changes a status column. Then you can create a widget based on that column.
Here is a demo on how to work with templates.
The only difference in your case will be to select “Unique values” in Step 3.

image1166×734 23.6 KB

Hope this helps. If you have any questions, you can always reach out at support@kolaai.com

posts.author: aolavarria (Adriana Olavarria)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having this same issue. And the Duplicates and Uniques app is entirely too expensive for us to get and pay a monthly fee for, just to have a single column counted. And the bar chart is totally unwieldy, when I’m managing a list of 200 apartment units, and I need to know how many individual units have a maintenance request.

posts.author: kjlshsu (Kevin Leach)
posts.cookedHtml:
Manually, one can click on filter and it shows “All of N rows” where N is your row count.
Obviously it would be good to be able to add this as a count under items.

posts.author: greentoothbrush (ian ross)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t think this works well if you move items between groups - the ID count changes i.e. it counts per group rather than per board.

posts.author: Bfly85 (Brianna Flynn)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Did you ever figure this out? I am looking for the same feature. I want to know how many unique clients we have utilizing a specific product.

posts.author: mowen (Mariela)
posts.cookedHtml: Wondering if there is a solution for this yet. I too am challenged with this situation.
